name: Cumin

supplementary_research.finding: RCT found cumin powder (3g/day for 3 months) in overweight women significantly reduced body weight, BMI, waist circumference, fat mass, fasting cholesterol, triglycerides, and LDL while increasing HDL, comparable to orlistat in effectiveness.

RCT in type 2 diabetic patients found cumin essential oil supplementation (25 mg, 3x daily) significantly reduced fasting blood glucose, HbA1c, and serum insulin levels over 8 weeks compared to placebo.

Review found cuminum cyminum demonstrates significant antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer activities. Thymoquinone and cuminaldehyde identified as primary bioactive compounds.
Preliminary evidence for weight management and blood glucose control. Active compounds include cuminaldehyde and thymoquinone. Generally well-tolerated at culinary doses.

culinary_pairings.source: Foundational spice combination in curries, hummus, and legume dishes
Essential in dals, falafel, hummus, and chilli
Core curry spice trio with coriander
Heat and earthiness in chilli con carne and curries
Classic pairing; often used in equal parts in spice blends
Base for salsas, curry sauces, and stewed dishes

seasonality: Available year-round as whole seeds or ground. Whole seeds retain flavour far longer than ground. Dry-roasting whole seeds before grinding intensifies flavour significantly. Store in airtight containers away from light.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: Primary volatile compound (20-30% of essential oil)
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Responsible for characteristic aroma. Antioxidant and antimicrobial activity. Well-absorbed orally.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: Present in essential oil (trace to moderate levels)
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Potent anti-inflammatory and anticancer compound. More concentrated in black cumin (Nigella sativa) but present in common cumin.
Very rich source of non-heme iron. Bioavailability enhanced by vitamin C from fresh chillies, tomatoes, or lemon.
Good source per serving; cofactor for antioxidant enzymes.

synergies.description: Traditional curry combination; complementary anti-inflammatory pathways
Cumin aids digestibility of legumes and is a universal legume spice across cultures
